proposal.verdict: VERDICT 095 (the +13 offset's nineteenth row — P082 -> V095)
proposal.claim_priced: GOTCHAS.md #4 verbatim: 'a stray checkbox edit (agents reflow markdown all the time) can only ever RE-QUEUE an owner action, never silently drop one'

pinned_behaviors.i_recognition: text.lstrip().startswith('⚑') and '**Owner:**' in text (ocq.py is_owner_row; derive_owner_queue.py lines 215/221 identical)
pinned_behaviors.ii_row_shape: ^- \[[ xX]\]\s+(.*)$ at line start (bullet '-', one space, single-char state, ']', whitespace)
pinned_behaviors.iii_fold_rule: indented non-empty lines fold into the OPEN item of the same regex class; a non-matching flush-left line closes the item; an indented line with NO open item is invisible
pinned_behaviors.iv_disposition: [—–-]\s*DONE\s+(\d{4})-(\d{2})-(\d{2}) (case-sensitive) on CHECKED rows only moves a row to Live; half-flipped cells still queue AND lint red (the both-marks rule)
pinned_behaviors.v_section_arming: gate section = first h2 matching ^##\s.*OWNER-GATE (IGNORECASE) to the next h2; ^KILL-CHECK:\s*(.*)$ heads the checkpoint line; checkpoints ARM only when the group has >= 1 recognized DONE row (once-live)
pinned_behaviors.lint_error_classes: half-flips both directions, invalid DONE date, defaultless flagged decision, no-H1, no-gate-section, no-rows, malformed KILL-CHECK token — every class conditioned on recognition

C0: 1. Package the zip and stage the listing draft.
3. Publish the listing once the gate clears.
- [ ] ⚑ **Owner:** click PUBLISH on the staged listing
Sequencing: the payout connect precedes the publish click.
applies to the storefront page too (blocking)

KILL-CHECK: ⏲ T+7 no-sale kill review · ⏲ T+30 price-drop checkpoint
corpus.C1_rule: C0 minus line 18 (the DONE row) — 20 lines
corpus.C2_rule: C0 with step 1 flagged + a '**…** (default)' marker: line 5 -> '1. ⚑ Package the zip and stage the listing draft — **stage first** (default).'

anchors_F3.position_law: OP3@r1 VANISH vs OP3@r2 MERGE, both pending 3; the merged WHAT contains both clicks' text (containment asserted)
anchors_F3.cascade_triple: OP1@r5, OP2@r5, OP4@r5 each land done 0 · armed 0 · lint 0

arm_r.draw_grammar: per trace EXACTLY 3 rng.randint draws in registered order — corpus in [0,2], grid cell in [1,26], salt in [1,1000] drawn-and-logged; one random.Random per seed; inapplicable/no-edit cells counted honestly as their own classes; NO statistical gate (gates are the 3N draw sentinel and exact reproducibility)

typed_contacts.C1: Arm B == Arm A on the three baseline tuples
typed_contacts.C2: Arm B == Arm A on all 26 grid cells and all four 2x2 cells
typed_contacts.C3: the two independently-written decision evaluators emit the same ruling token
typed_contacts.C4: Arm R: A == B on every trace, draw counters == 3N exactly (60,000 / 24,000), and the class-stream digests reproduce

decision_rule.order: REJECT -> INVALID -> APPROVE -> NULL, REJECT evaluated FIRST
decision_rule.REJECT: R1 (the true sentence survives: the disposition 2x2 exact, zero silent cells) AND R2 (the 26-cell census exact at {18,3,4,1} with the SILENT-LOSSY list verbatim AND the lint-downstream law: every SILENT cell lint 0 AND manual 0) AND R3 (cascade triple done 0/armed 0/lint 0 AND C1 parsed 2/armed 0) AND R4 (OP13 guarded manual>=1 lint>=1 while R2 row breaks are silent, AND the conservation-lint repair reproduces its registered catch census)
decision_rule.APPROVE: zero SILENT-LOSSY cells on the registered grid — arithmetically excluded by the OP1@r1 pencil cell; mutually exclusive with REJECT
decision_rule.NULL: anything else; named axes: source-semantics mismatch, corpus-convention, twin-fidelity mismatch
decision_rule.strict_inequality_margin: SILENT-LOSSY >= 15 in the evaluator carries margin 3 below the registered 18

classification_taxonomy.LOSSY: pending < baseline OR decisions < baseline OR done < baseline OR armed < baseline (a pending click, a queue decision, a DONE row, or an armed checkpoint LOST)

repair_arm.looser_net: whole-file: (owner-ish) any bullet-ish ('- ', '* ', '-[', '*[' after lstrip) or ⚑-bearing line whose markup-stripped (chars *_` removed, lowercased) text contains 'owner:'; (default-ish) any numbered step containing '(default'; (kill-ish) any ⏲ line whose lowercased text contains 'kill'
repair_arm.fires_when: strict recognition counts fewer than the looser net on any of the three nets (strict owner rows = pending+done; strict decisions = recognized flagged steps; strict kill = recognized ^KILL-CHECK: lines in the gate section)

vacancy_derived_disclosures.note: sim-chosen realizations of shape-registered slots — disclosed here BEFORE the runner, never match claims; every REGISTERED value above is a match claim and must reproduce exactly
vacancy_derived_disclosures.corpus_wording: the registration pins C0's structure verbatim-by-constraint (21 lines; the DONE row at line 18 — C1 is a line-18 deletion; 3 steps with step 2 the flagged default-marked decision; 4 pending ⚑ **Owner:** rows with r4 carrying the indented '(blocking)' continuation; the non-owner seat row after the DONE row; KILL-CHECK with 2 ⏲ checkpoints at line 21; baseline tuple echoed above) but the drafter's literal WHAT wording is not quoted in the proposal — the wording here is a sim-chosen realization; the witness that it realizes the registered corpus exactly is the F3 anchor battery INCLUDING both registered Arm-R class-stream digests, which are functions of the full 78-pair class map
vacancy_derived_disclosures.target_resolution: operator targets are pinned as C0 1-based line numbers and applied at the SAME line number in every corpus (C1's deletion shifts content up through them); a target line number beyond the corpus length is INAPPLICABLE; an edit whose anchor substring is absent leaves the line unchanged = NO-EDIT
vacancy_derived_disclosures.two_by_two_realization: the disposition 2x2 is taken on r1 with valid date 2026-07-14 for the DONE legs (check via OP8's edit; DONE via appending ' — DONE 2026-07-14'; both for the retire cell)
vacancy_derived_disclosures.class_stream_digest_procedure: sha256 of the UTF-8 bytes of ''.join(class labels in trace order, exact spellings SILENT-LOSSY/ALARMED-LOSSY/ALARMED-SAFE/SILENT-REGRADE/NO-EDIT/INAPPLICABLE), hexdigest truncated to the first 12 chars
vacancy_derived_disclosures.salt_logging: the per-trace salt draw is logged in aggregate (sum + first 5 salts per seed) in results.json; the salt does not enter classification or the class stream
vacancy_derived_disclosures.presentation_target: seed 20261724 shuffles the display order of the 26-cell census table in stdout (presentation leg only)
vacancy_derived_disclosures.observable_tuple: steps, decisions, decisions-no-default, pending, done, non-owner, parsed, armed, blocked, manual, lint, pending texts, done dates — plus explicit merged/vanished row accounting for the F1 conservation identity
